history: The client has long-standing strained relationships with both parents, often feeling judged and disapproved of. There is a background of inadequate parental support during education, and the mother is described as 'broken' with a history of a past affair, which may have contributed to family instability and mistrust. The mother's health condition is also a source of concern. These dynamics have likely fostered persistent feelings of inadequacy, anxiety, and emotional distance within the family.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If I am not perfect or do not meet my parents' expectations, they will judge and disapprove of me, and I will never be accepted. I must always be on guard to avoid criticism. If I show any vulnerability, it will confirm their negative view of me.
intermediate_belief_depression: No matter what I do, I will never be able to change my parents' opinion of me, so there is no point in trying to connect or be myself around them.
coping_strategies: The client tends to withdraw emotionally and may minimize communication with their parents to avoid conflict or judgment. They also engage in anticipatory anxiety, rehearsing conversations or planning how to manage distress during family gatherings. There is a tendency to seek reassurance from others or distract themselves to cope with feelings of inadequacy.
situation: Preparing to attend a Father's Day lunch with parents.
auto_thought: They're just going to judge me again and see all the ways I don't measure up. No matter what I do, it won't be good enough for them.
behavior: I rehearse what I might say to avoid criticism. I consider ways to keep the conversation superficial. I feel tempted to withdraw or leave early if things get uncomfortable.
